note: The offline embedding provider uses deterministic BLAKE3-derived vectors, not semantic embeddings. Confidence scores are structural/vector-space proximity, NOT semantic similarity. All confidence values are low (<0.4) for all tasks because: (1) the current seed corpus is parsing primitives (digit, integer, whitespace, etc.) that are semantically distant from all 8 tasks; (2) the offline provider produces low-discrimination vectors. After bootstrapping with a real embedding model (e.g. all-MiniLM-L6-v2), confidence scores will be meaningful. The timer-handle atom (closes #454) is seeded in this WI but requires registry rebuild to appear in query results.

tasks.query_intent: cache key-value pairs with TTL expiration and LRU eviction, O(1) get and set operations using doubly linked list and hash map
tasks.gap_reason: No atom in the current corpus is semantically close to LRU+TTL cache data structures. The doubly-linked-list and Map-based eviction pattern has no seed atom. The seed corpus (20+1 parsing primitives) is purely text-parsing focused.
tasks.gap_notes: Filing as coverage gap. Seed atoms needed: lru-node (doubly-linked-list node), time-stamp-provider (monotonic timestamp for TTL). Out of scope for WI-460 — these are separate seed corpus WIs.

tasks.query_intent: parse CSV data with quoted fields per RFC 4180, handle embedded newlines and escaped quotes
tasks.gap_reason: The CSV parsing primitive atoms (comma, bracket, peek-char) are semantically related but confidence is below 0.5 with the offline embedding provider. With a real embedding model, the comma/bracket atoms should score higher. Not a registry gap per se, but a threshold gap.
tasks.gap_notes: Comma and bracket atoms exist and are relevant to CSV parsing. The offline embedding vector quality prevents them from surfacing at confidence >= 0.5. With a real embedding provider, this task should have coverage.

tasks.query_intent: debounce a function call with delayed execution, cancel pending timer with clearTimeout, flush immediately
tasks.gap_reason: The timer-handle atom (setTimeout/clearTimeout closure pattern) is seeded by WI-460 (closes #454) but the .yakcc/registry.sqlite has not been rebuilt with the new seed yet. After 'yakcc bootstrap', this task should have the timer-handle atom as a relevant candidate.
tasks.gap_notes: timer-handle atom seeded at packages/seeds/src/blocks/timer-handle/. Requires registry rebuild to appear in query results. This is NOT a gap — the atom exists, the registry just needs reseeding.

tasks.query_intent: compute minimum edit distance between two strings using dynamic programming memoization
tasks.gap_reason: Dynamic programming / memoization patterns have no seed atom in the current corpus. Levenshtein requires a 2D matrix iteration primitive that does not exist.
tasks.gap_notes: Filed: https://github.com/cneckar/yakcc/issues/465 — seed a memoize atom (function memoize pattern) for levenshtein/topological-sort coverage.

tasks.query_intent: topological sort of a directed acyclic graph using Kahn's algorithm with in-degree queue
tasks.gap_reason: Graph traversal / queue-drain patterns have no seed atom. Kahn's algorithm requires in-degree map, neighbor adjacency, and queue management primitives.
tasks.gap_notes: Filed: https://github.com/cneckar/yakcc/issues/466 — seed a queue-drain atom for topological sort coverage.

tasks.query_intent: resolve JSON pointer per RFC 6901 navigating nested objects and arrays with escape sequences tilde zero tilde one
tasks.gap_reason: The positionStep atom is structurally related (position-based navigation) but doesn't provide the JSON pointer escape-decode pattern (~0/~1). String navigation primitives exist but not JSON-specific ones.
tasks.gap_notes: Filed: https://github.com/cneckar/yakcc/issues/467 — seed a JSON pointer token splitter / escape decoder atom. The position-step and string-from-position atoms provide partial coverage.

tasks.query_intent: base64 encode binary data using RFC 4648 alphabet with padding character equals sign
tasks.gap_reason: Base64 encoding requires a 64-character alphabet lookup table and 3-byte to 4-character grouping. The ascii-char / ascii-digit-set atoms are tangentially related but don't provide the base64 alphabet.
tasks.gap_notes: Filed: https://github.com/cneckar/yakcc/issues/468 — seed a base64-alphabet lookup atom for base64-encode coverage.

tasks.query_intent: parse semantic version and check if version satisfies a range constraint following semver specification
tasks.gap_reason: Semver parsing requires integer parsing (exists: integer atom) and dot/hyphen tokenization. The signed-integer and digit atoms are relevant for parsing major.minor.patch components.
tasks.gap_notes: Filed: https://github.com/cneckar/yakcc/issues/469 — seed a semver component parser atom composing integer + dot-separator. The existing integer/digit atoms provide foundation but not the version-range grammar.

action_items: Rebuild registry with 'yakcc bootstrap' to include timer-handle atom (debounce coverage improvement)
Note: With a real embedding provider (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 or similar), PARTIAL tasks may achieve coverage at 0.5+ threshold
Note: FULL coverage requires dedicated seed atoms per task domain — out of scope for WI-460
